name: Will my doors need to be trimmed after installing new flooring?
headline: Will my doors need to be trimmed after installing new flooring?

mainEntity.name: Will my doors need to be trimmed after installing new flooring?
mainEntity.text: Will my doors need to be trimmed after installing new flooring?

acceptedAnswer.text: In many cases, yes, you will need to trim or adjust doors after new flooring is installed, especially if the new floor is thicker than what you had before. When you add height to a floor, interior doors can drag, rub, or not close at all. Exterior doors and closet doors can also be affected. During the planning stage, ask your installer how much height the new floor and underlayment will add compared to your old floor. If doors end up too low, they usually have to be removed, trimmed at the bottom, and rehung. This can often be done with a circular saw or a specialized door trimmer. In some jobs, this is included in the flooring estimate; in others, it is extra or handled by a carpenter. Another detail is undercutting door jambs and casings so the flooring can slide underneath for a cleaner look. This is common with hard surface floors and gives a more professional finish. A common mistake is forgetting to budget time and money for door work, especially in older homes where floors are already uneven. Talk about doors, transitions to other flooring, and thresholds before the job starts so you are not surprised when a bedroom or bathroom door suddenly sticks after installation.
